---
title: Project Init
description: How to create and initialize a new Agent project with the CLI
---

# Project Init

The `city agent create` command creates a new Agent project with the minimum files needed to run and stores agent config in the CLI global DB.

## Basic usage

```bash
city agent create my-project
```

This creates:

```text
my-project/
├── PROFILE.md
├── SOUL.md
├── .downcity/
└── .env
```

## Generated files

- `PROFILE.md` — the agent's visible role and response style. See [Profile](/en/docs/cli/profile).
- `SOUL.md` — deeper, long-term operating principles. See [Profile](/en/docs/cli/profile).
- `.downcity/` — runtime data directory (sessions, logs, cache).
- `.env` — environment variables for the project.
- Agent config in the CLI global DB — agent ID, model binding, startup options, and plugin options.

## Options

```bash
# Allow overwriting existing Agent config
city agent create my-project --force
```

## After init

1. Edit `PROFILE.md` and `SOUL.md` to shape behavior.
2. Use `city agent config` or the interactive `city agent` manager for model, port, and chat account binding.
3. Run `city agent start` to start the agent.

Continue with:

- [Project Structure](/en/docs/cli/project)
- [legacy downcity.json](/en/docs/cli/downcity-json)
